topic_title: Pattern recognition — how machines find rules in repeated data
profile_note: No suggested_profile declared in corpus frontmatter or topic.manifest.json (expected_delivery_minutes: null). Topic exhibits deep_signals per artifact-mix.json classification_signals: 6 Core Concepts subsections (5.1–5.6), 5-step Implementation section, Real-World Patterns section, Best Practices section. Conservative floor is 'standard' (3000-word corpus minimum). Atomic disqualified by presence of Implementation and Real-World Patterns sections.
word_count_basis: Carried forward from run 2 pre-mutation count; only change between run 2 corpus and current corpus is removal of bold markers from 'model drift' at line 124 (net 4 chars removed, zero word-count impact). Count treated as stable at ~2587.

section_3_learning_objectives.note: 6 bullet objectives covering rule-based vs pattern-recognition, training, feature/label/classification, probabilistic outputs, and spec writing implications.

section_4_introduction.note: Three paragraphs: connects to prior Week 1 pattern-recognition surface intro, poses the core question (how does a machine do it?), bridges to upcoming A1 assessment.

section_5_core_concepts.note: Six subsections (5.1 rule-based vs pattern-recognition; 5.2 training on labeled data; 5.3 features; 5.4 classification; 5.5 probabilistic outputs; 5.6 training–deployment gap). Each subsection substantive.

section_10_key_takeaways.note: 5 bullets covering all main concepts of the topic.

sequence_drift.concepts_introduced_in_prior_topics: All empty ([]) per sequence.index.json. Only title-derivable concepts available from prior curriculum: computation (1.1), deterministic systems (1.2), probabilistic systems (1.3), pattern recognition surface intro (1.4), decomposition (1.5), abstraction (1.6), pseudocode (1.7), flowcharts (1.8), algorithmic thinking (1.9–1.11), problem statement (1.12), testable/bounded/observable/actionable spec properties (2.1), bad-vs-good spec (2.2), inputs/expected-outputs/failure-conditions/six-component checklist (2.3).

forward_references_soft.context: "...measured on a held-out validation set of 500 labeled emails not seen during training."
forward_references_soft.note: Technical ML term used without plain-language definition at first appearance. Not formally introduced until later in the sequence. Appears inside a specification example — not taught as a concept — so this is a soft forward-reference finding, not a hard block. Suggested fix: add a parenthetical gloss, e.g. '(a separate batch of emails not used during training)'.

sequence_drift.model_drift_status: RESOLVED — line 124 text is 'This is called model drift and is covered in a later module.' — bare name, unbolded, one sentence, explicit deferral pointer. Correct mention-and-defer form. Prior run 2 hard fail on this item is cleared.

length_sanity.detail: ~2,587 words is 13.8% below the standard-profile corpus floor of 3,000 words. This falls within the 30% hard-fail buffer (floor × 0.70 = 2,100 words). No hard block on length. Soft finding: corpus is slightly below target range — modest expansion of Implementation (§6) and Best Practices (§8) would bring it to standard range. Builder must also declare 'suggested_profile' in corpus frontmatter (currently missing).

findings.detail: ~2,587 words vs standard-profile floor 3,000. Within 30% hard-fail buffer. Surfaces to HITL #2 as an advisory — author can accept or request expansion.

topic_scope.headline: Pattern recognition — how machines find rules in repeated data

soft_findings.detail: Used in a spec-writing example without definition. Not taught at depth, does not constitute teaching an out-of-scope concept. Same instance flagged under sequence_drift and beginner_readability.

topic_scope.model_drift_resolution: RESOLVED — prior hard fail cleared. Line 124: 'This is called model drift and is covered in a later module.' — bare name (no bold), one sentence, explicit 'covered in a later module' pointer. Satisfies mention-and-defer form per artifact-mix.json scope_discipline rules.
topic_scope.other_scope_notes: Expert system (§5.1): defined inline as synonym for rule-based system — within scope. Feature selection/extraction (§5.3): named together in one sentence, appropriately within the 'find rules' headline scope. Confidence score: introduced here, within scope. No neural-network internals, gradient descent, backpropagation, or other advanced ML concepts taught.

feature selection / feature extraction.note: Defined implicitly by surrounding context ('Choosing which features to measure... This step is called...'); adequate for beginners.

findings.note: 'validation set' used in a specification example without plain-language gloss. A beginner at week 2 lacks this vocabulary. Suggested fix: add parenthetical '(a separate batch of emails set aside and not used during training)' immediately after first use.

beginner_readability.detail: Readability is strong overall. All primary technical terms defined on first use with bold + plain-English meanings. Sentences are short, active-voice, and learner-addressed throughout. No jargon dumps detected — no paragraph introduces 3+ undefined terms simultaneously. No hedging phrases. 'false positive' at line 186 (exercise) presents plain meaning first ('ended up in spam by mistake'), term in parentheses — acceptable form. 'precision' used in section 8 examples can be read as everyday English ('exactness') in context — borderline but not a distinct finding. One distinct soft finding (validation set) — below ≥3 threshold for hard-fail escalation.

soft_findings_summary.detail: ~2,587 words vs standard-profile floor 3,000. Within 30% hard-fail buffer. Advisory only.
Corpus frontmatter missing 'suggested_profile' field. Builder defect — surfaces at HITL #2.
'validation set' used at line 115 without plain-language definition. Appears in a spec example — not taught as a concept. Add a parenthetical gloss. Surfaces at HITL #2.

verdict: PASS — all hard-fail checks clear. The prior run 2 hard fail (scope_creep: 'model drift' bolded in §5.6) is resolved — line 124 now reads as bare name with explicit deferral pointer, correct mention-and-defer form. The prior length hard fail (run 1: ~2100 words) is not a hard fail at the current ~2587-word count (13.8% below standard floor, within 30% buffer). Three soft findings surface to HITL #2: (a) corpus is ~413 words below the standard-profile floor — advisory expansion of §6/§8 recommended; (b) 'suggested_profile' missing from corpus frontmatter; (c) 'validation set' used at line 115 without definition — add a brief parenthetical gloss. No blocking issues remain.
